holdings.note: SpaceX — IPO'd Jun 2026; FMP may not yet cover earnings/dividend dates for a brand-new listing.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

INTC.read: INTC is up 10.5% today and 14.4% on the week with no dated company catalyst on the board — that's a momentum move, not a fundamentals-anchored one, and today is triple witching which can amplify and then unwind index/options flow. With no earnings or ex-div date to anchor a hold, the asymmetry on a stretched 8-share position tilts toward harvesting some of the spike rather than chasing.
INTC.next_catalyst: 2026-06-19 — Triple witching (options/futures expiry, flow event, not company-specific)
INTC.bull: Momentum + short-cover dynamics into expiry can extend the squeeze through the close and into next week.
INTC.bear: No company catalyst means the +14% move is flow-driven; post-witching unwind plus mean reversion can give most of it back.

NVDA.read: No dated company catalyst on the board for NVDA — next earnings isn't scheduled in your feed, so there's no binary print to lean into. The tape is constructive (+2.8% on the day, +2.7% on the week) at $210, and today happens to be triple witching, which can amplify intraday moves but isn't a fundamental driver. Without an earnings date or guide event, this is a hold-and-watch until either an AI-cycle datapoint or the next print gets confirmed.
NVDA.next_catalyst: 2026-06-19 — Triple witching (options expiry, flow event, not fundamental)
NVDA.bull: Trend intact, leadership name reasserting on the week with no catalyst overhang into expiry flows.
NVDA.bear: No dated fundamental catalyst means the position rides macro/positioning; witching-day strength can unwind into next week.

ORCL.read: No dated company catalyst on the board for ORCL right now — just triple witching today and again in September, which is positioning noise, not a fundamental driver. Price is barely changed on the day and week ($184.31, +0.4% / +0.2%), so there's no stretched move to fade or weakness to press. The actionable window is the next earnings print (typically mid-Sep for Oracle's FQ1), where OCI growth and RPO guide will reset the AI-infra narrative that's been carrying this name.
ORCL.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — triple witching (no company catalyst on calendar; FQ1 earnings typically land mid-September, not yet dated here)
ORCL.bull: OCI backlog and AI capacity bookings keep compounding, and any pre-earnings drift higher into the September print rewards holders.
ORCL.bear: Capex intensity and margin pressure from the AI buildout could disappoint at the next print, and a flat tape into witching offers no edge either way.

NOK.read: NOK is down 2.3% today and 4.3% on the week with no dated company catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div in hand — so there's no specific event creating an add/trim window here. The nearest scheduled event is triple witching today, which is a flow/positioning event, not a NOK-specific driver. Without a dated catalyst, the honest read is to let the next earnings date or a dividend declaration set the clock before leaning in.
NOK.next_catalyst: 2026-06-19 — Triple witching (macro flow event, not NOK-specific)
NOK.bull: Quality network-equipment exposure pulling back on no news; a dated earnings or dividend confirmation would reset the setup.
NOK.bear: Drift lower with no near-term company catalyst to break the downtrend and no visible support from a print or payout.

NBIS.read: NBIS is up 29% in five days into today's triple witching with no company-specific dated catalyst on the tape — that's a stretched tape running into a known liquidity/gamma unwind event, not a fundamental setup. Without an earnings or product date to anchor the move, the asymmetry favors taking some off into the strength and letting the post-witching tape define the next entry. If it holds the breakout through next week, you re-engage; if dealer flows reverse, you've already booked the easy part.
NBIS.bull: Momentum leader with no overhead supply; post-witching the squeeze can extend into the next AI capex print cycle.
NBIS.bear: +29% in a week into triple witching with no dated company catalyst — pure positioning unwind risk once gamma rolls off.

KO.read: Tiny 5-share KO position with no dated company catalysts on the board — no earnings or ex-div date in the feed — and the tape is just softly bleeding (-3.2% over five sessions). With nothing binary in front of it, today's triple witching is the only near-term event, and that's a flow/volatility tell, not a KO-specific catalyst. Wait for the next earnings or ex-dividend date to populate before defining an add or trim window.
KO.bull: Defensive staple drifting lower into no event risk — weakness without a catalyst is usually the better entry on quality compounders.
KO.bear: No dated company catalyst means no forcing function; the slow bleed can continue on rates/FX with nothing to break the trend.

SPCX.read: SPCX is a brand-new June 2026 listing with no confirmed earnings or dividend dates in the feed yet — there's no dated company catalyst to anchor an add/trim window against. Today is also triple witching, which can amplify noise in a thinly-seasoned IPO without telling you anything fundamental. Best posture is to let the first earnings date get confirmed and let the post-IPO lockup/float picture clarify before sizing decisions.
SPCX.next_catalyst: 2026-06-19 — Triple witching (no company-specific catalyst yet on the tape)
SPCX.bull: First-mover liquid SpaceX exposure; scarcity premium and index/ETF inclusion flows can dominate price action in the early post-IPO window.
SPCX.bear: No earnings history, unknown lockup expiry, and IPO-era volatility — triple witching today can magnify supply/demand air pockets in a name with thin float.

disclaimer: Forward earnings dates are third-party aggregator estimates, not company-confirmed — confirm against the company's IR release (~2 weeks ahead). Dividends, macro prints and witching are scheduled/confirmed. Not investment advice.
